markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define discard/refine/temporal-qualify/granularity-qualify/merge outcomes and required receipts. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: ConflictResolutionAgentJob leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ConflictResolutionAgentJob is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 91c204e6; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-123 requires a receipt-backed ConflictResolutionAgentJob defining discard/refine/temporal-qualify/granularity-qualify/merge outcomes with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code delivers a real Postgres-backed typed job record (migration 0242), enumerates all five outcomes with a NOT NULL EventLedger receipt FK and a CHECK enforcing kept/discarded shape, and proves a discard resolution plus fail-closed negatives against real managed PostgreSQL.
findings: Migration 0242_knowledge_memory_conflict_jobs.sql:69-105 creates knowledge_memory_conflict_resolution_jobs with all five MT-required outcomes CHECK-constrained ('discard','refine','temporal_qualify','granularity_qualify','merge'), a NOT NULL resolution_receipt_event_id FK -> kernel_event_ledger(event_id) (receipt-backed per spec), and chk_kmcrj_claim_shape enforcing kept+discarded for discard/merge and kept for the qualify/refine outcomes.

storage/knowledge_memory.rs:1134-1177 record_conflict_resolution_job validates kept-claim-required and discard/merge-requires-discarded in Rust before the DB CHECK backstop; ConflictResolutionOutcome enum (1062-1100) covers all five outcomes with as_str/from_db round-trip.

tests/knowledge_memory_conflict_tests.rs:133-217 resolution_job_records_outcome_with_receipt appends a REAL kernel_event_ledger event (append_kernel_event) and records a discard resolution job against managed PostgreSQL, asserting KCRJ- id, outcome, and receipt linkage; two negatives (discard without discarded claim; resolution without kept claim) assert StorageError::Validation fail-closed.

tests/knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:8-9 states 'Real PostgreSQL only. No SQLite, no mocks.'; MemoryFixture::setup returns None and SKIPs loudly when PG binaries absent — sub_rule_2 real-resource touch satisfied.

knowledge_memory/conflict.rs MT-123 path re-exports record_conflict_resolution_job / list_conflict_resolution_jobs / ConflictResolutionJob; no todo!/unimplemented!/Unavailable/deferred escape reachable from the resolution path (only grep hit is fixtures.rs:8 comment 'none of them mock or stub').

Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:2704 (sec 2.3.13.11) requires KnowledgeClaim conflict refs + resolution receipts; impl aligns — resolution is receipt-backed and reuses the 0137 knowledge_claim_conflicts substrate that moves backing claims to 'conflicted'.

Coverage aside (non-blocking): only the Discard outcome is positively round-tripped in a test; refine/temporal_qualify/granularity_qualify/merge rely on the shared enum + DB CHECK + Rust validation (the shape constraint is exercised via negatives) but are not individually inserted/read-back. This is a test-coverage gap, not a realism or correctness defect.
Down-migration 0242.down.sql cleanly drops the three tables and deregisters the schema_registry families — rollback path present.
Governance-paper aside: lifecycle.completed_by is null and verdict not yet recorded — expected; not a blocking reason.
evidence_checked: MT-123.json contract (scope, acceptance_criteria, constraints, red_team, lifecycle.implementation_commit=91c204e6, evidence)
grep for placeholder/escape variants across src/knowledge_memory
SPEC_CURRENT v02.194 02-system-architecture.md sec 2.3.13.11 (KnowledgeClaim conflict refs + resolution receipts)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
